name: Stir Fried Pork Slices in Wine Sauce
recipeInstructions:
Prepare Paste: Use mortar and pestle to pulverize cooked rice. Combine with sugar, yeast, soy sauce, and warm water. Let stand in a warm place for 30 minutes to activate yeast. Authentic wine lees paste is not available in the U.S., so this substitute is provided. You can add wet bean cheese for a sharper flavor.
Braise Pork: Slice pork butt across the grain into strips, 1-inch by 3-inch by 1/2-inch thick. Heat peanut oil in a wok until it begins to smoke. Add some of the pork to hot oil; stir-fry pieces until they lose their pinkness; repeat in batches until all pork is browned. Next, add garlic to the wok; stir briefly. Pour in wine lees paste, rice vinegar, sherry, and stock; bring to slow boil; add pork slices. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove pork without sauce to a large platter. Cool pork. Cooling is essential so that it will deep-fry properly. Reserve sauce in a small pan. You can hold pork for several hours if you wish to braise it in advance.
Deep Fry Pork: Heat deep frying oil in a wok. While the oil is heating, beat egg yolks with water; set out bread crumbs on a platter. Dip pork pieces in egg mixture, then bread crumbs, to thoroughly cover. When oil reaches deep-frying temperature (375 degrees), slip in a slice of pork as a test: pork should lightly brown in about 1 minute. Place 6 pork slices on a Chinese strainer, and lower into the oil, strainer and all. Check in 2 minutes (browning should take slightly longer than the test because the strainer cools the oil). If you prefer to fry in larger batches, use more oil. Remove fried pork to a warm platter, uncovered.
Finish: Reheat sauce, and pour over pork just before serving.
